Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents an event created by a vendor.

status.description: The approval status of the event.

Vendor.title: Vendor
Vendor.description: Represents a vendor who creates events.

id.description: Unique identifier for the vendor, same as the user ID.

paystackRecipientId.description: Paystack Recipient ID for vendor payouts.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user who can purchase tickets and attend events.

roles.description: The roles assigned to the user.

Ticket.title: Ticket
Ticket.description: Represents a ticket purchased by a user for an event.

isUsed.description: Indicates if the ticket has been used.

tier.description: The name of the tier for tiered events.

attendeeName.description: The name of the person attending, if different from the buyer.

Admin.title: Admin
Admin.description: Represents an administrator user with management privileges.

id.description: Unique identifier for the admin user.

TicketTemplate.title: TicketTemplate
TicketTemplate.description: Represents a template for designing event tickets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ticket template.

design.description: JSON representing the design elements of the ticket template.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification for a user.

userId.description: The ID of the user this notification belongs to.

description.description: The main content of the notification.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the notification was created.

read.description: Whether the user has read the notification.

link.description: An optional link to navigate to when the notification is clicked.

Wallet.title: Wallet
Wallet.description: Represents a user's wallet for storing funds.

id.description: Unique identifier for the wallet, same as the user ID.

balance.description: The current balance of the wallet.

currency.description: The currency of the wallet balance.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single transaction in a user's wallet.

walletId.description: The ID of the wallet this transaction belongs to.

amount.description: The amount of the transaction. Can be positive (credit) or negative (debit).

date.description: The timestamp when the transaction occurred.

description.description: A brief description of the transaction.

relatedEntityId.description: The ID of the related entity (e.g., Ticket ID, Payout ID).

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Only the user themselves (or an admin) should be able to read/write their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores vendor profiles. Only the vendor themselves (or an admin) should be able to read/write their own profile.

params.name: vendorId
params.description: The unique ID of the vendor.

definition.description: Stores events. This collection is public for reads, allowing anyone to view event details. Write access is restricted to the event's vendor owner or an admin.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique ID of the event.

definition.description: Stores tickets purchased by users. Tickets are nested under the user's profile to indicate ownership.  Includes denormalized `userId` and `eventId` for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who purchased the ticket.

params.name: ticketId
params.description: The unique ID of the ticket.

definition.description: Stores event categories.  Accessible by all users for reading, but only admins can create/update/delete.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the event category.

definition.description: Stores ticket templates. Accessible by all users for reading, but only admins can create/update/delete.

params.name: ticketTemplateId
params.description: The unique ID of the ticket template.

definition.description: Indicates admin privileges. The existence of a document at this path grants admin rights to the user. No data within the document is necessary; existence is sufficient.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user with admin privileges.

definition.description: Stores notifications for a specific user. Notifications are nested under the user's profile for ownership-based access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique ID of the notification.

definition.description: Stores wallet information for a user. A user can only access their own wallet.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Stores transaction history for a user's wallet. Users can only read their own transactions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization of authorization-related data, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. Structural Segregation is applied to ensure homogeneous security postures within collections.  Access Modeling follows consistent patterns for private and collaborative data.

Specifically:

*   User data is stored under `/users/{userId}` allowing path based ownership.
*   Vendor data is stored under `/vendors/{vendorId}`, enabling vendor-specific data access controls.
*   Events are now stored in a root `/events/{eventId}` collection. This makes querying for all events much simpler (e.g., for the public homepage) without requiring complex collection group queries. Each event document contains a `vendorId` field to maintain the relationship and for use in security rules.
*   Tickets are stored under `/users/{userId}/tickets/{ticketId}`, providing strong ownership and simplifies access control.
*   Admin users are managed via the `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection. The existence of a document at this path grants admin privileges.
*   User notifications are stored under `/users/{userId}/notifications/{notificationId}` to ensure privacy and ownership.
*   Wallets are stored at `/wallets/{userId}` to associate them directly with a user.
*   Transactions are a subcollection of a wallet at `/wallets/{userId}/transactions/{transactionId}` for clear ownership.

The structure facilitates secure list operations (QAPs) by ensuring that collections contain data with similar access control requirements. For example, anyone can list events from the `/events` collection, but only an owner or admin can write to a specific event document. The admin role can list all vendors and all events.
